methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies, a Series G climate and energy company founded by Norman Rostoker and Michl Binderbauer, is valued at $6.0B post-money following $1.3B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 9 comparable companies: 1 public (LNZA) and 8 private-stage firms including Helion, Form Energy, Sila Nanotechnologies, and Watershed, spanning Series F through late-stage rounds in the same sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis anchors to TAE's $6.0B last post-money valuation and applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement, yielding $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Helion at $5.4B, Form Energy at $3.5B, Sila at $3.3B, Watershed at $1.8B) produces a median of $3.4B, with a range of $1.8B to $5.4B. The peer-median sits below TAE's current valuation, reflecting either a premium for TAE's capital efficiency relative to peers or execution risk embedded in comparable valuations.

The triangulated range spans $2.9B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $5.5B. The current $6.0B valuation sits within the upper half of this range, supported by stage-progression logic but elevated relative to peer medians. Key uncertainties include the timing and trajectory of revenue inflection and relative capital efficiency versus the peer cohort.
